title: Park Sang-hyun made a strong start to win his third career championship in the GS Caltex Maekyung Op.. - 매일경제
content:
Park Sang-hyun made a strong start to win his third career championship in the GS Caltex Maekyung Open. He showed his fighting spirit even when it was difficult to play normally in the aftermath of a back injury, showing the dignity of a veteran. Park Sang-hyun shot a 1-under 70 in the first round of the 45th GS Caltex Maekyung Open held at Nam Seoul CC in Seongnam, Gyeonggi Province on April 30. Park Sang-hyun, who saved one stroke by combining five birdies, one bogey and one triple bogey, tied for 34th with Choi Seung-bin and Choi Min-chul. She is five shots behind Lee Tae-hee, the lone lead. Park Sang-hyun, who ranks No. 1 in total prize money with 14 wins in the Korea Professional Golfers Association (KPGA) Tour, is a sign of Korean men's golf. He also has a special relationship with the GS Caltex Maekyung Open, which is called the "Korea's Masters." Park Sang-hyun, the 2016 and 2018 winner of the tournament, is competing with Lee Tae-hee and Kim Bi-oh for the most-ever winner title. Park Sang-hyun, who showed off his robustness by winning two games last year, was fully prepared to spend the 2026 season successfully. However, he was hampered by injuries suffered ahead of the opening DB Insurance Promy Open. Park withdrew due to back pain and also missed last week's Woori Finance Championship. Ahead of the opening of the GS Caltex Maekyung Open, the participation itself was uncertain. On the first day, Park Sang-hyun wrote a message on his left-handed glove to golf fans and officials concerned about him, expressing his current physical condition and gratitude. Park Sang-hyun said, "So many people asked me if my back was okay, so I prepared a surprise event," adding, "I'm not in my best physical condition, but I've improved enough to play." I will prepare well and finish the GS Caltex Maekyung Open in a good mood," he stressed. Park Sang-hyun, who started the game in a good mood on the first day with birdies on the first and third holes, faced a crisis at the par-5 fourth hole. It was expected that he would put the ball on the green with his third shot and score par without much difficulty. However, he made a mistake of making a five-putt on the green and wrote down a triple bogey. Park lost three strokes in one hole, but Park remained calm. Park Sang-hyun, who changed the atmosphere with birdies on the 7th and 9th holes, showed not bad performance even in the second half when tricky holes were arranged. He wrote down one birdie and one bogey each, and finished the first day at 1-under par. Park Sang-hyun said, "I'm satisfied with the whole thing except for the fourth hole. "I don't think I was humble at the fourth hole, where I recorded a triple bogey," he said. "It was an opportunity to regain my mind." "I will do my best in the rest of the round with the thought of playing South Seoul CC for the first time," he said. The goal of this tournament is to win the championship. Park Sang-hyun said, "I hate not performing well more than being sick," adding, "I'm happy to participate in the competition, but I really want to do well at the GS Caltex Maekyung Open. I hope the day will come when I win again in front of the cloud crowd," he said. Following last year, Park Sang-hyun will participate in the GS Caltex Maekyung Open Top Golfer's cherished item event this year. We have prepared a special gift for golf fans visiting the South Seoul CC. Park Sang-hyun said, "I prepared my first professional championship putter and wedge last year, and this year I'm going to give a caddie bag as a gift. "I hid a secret gift that the person who received it would be surprised in the caddy bag," he said with a smile. ※ This article was translated using AI technology for reader convenience. title: Lunchbreak: 5 Vikings Draft Choices Spotlighted in ESPN’s Top 100 ‘Picks, Steals, Fits’ - Minnesota Vikings
content:
Two-hundred-and-fifty-seven college football standouts realized life-long dreams last weekend. In the days since the 2026 NFL Draft concluded, we've watched hundreds more join teams as undrafted free agents, including 19 on the Vikings. We've also observed initial report cards from external draftniks and noted brief assessments earlier this week of Minnesota investing twice in run-game reinforcements. Another post-draft recap worth highlighting came out Wednesday: ESPN draft analyst Matt Miller's 100 best picks, fits and steals of the event — and to spoil the surprise, Minnesota was a recurring awardee. The Vikings are one of five teams on Miller's sheet with five "favorite" selections last weekend, along with the Chargers, Colts, Jets — whose three first-round picks were ranked 5, 6 and 22 — and Ravens. That's the majority of Minnesota's nine-player draft class, in case you hate math. The team's fourth pick and first offensive player chosen this year, former Northwestern tackle Caleb Tiernan, appeared first for the Vikings at 34 in Miller's ranking. Tiernan was Miller's 61st prospect available and was drafted 97th overall, confirming Minnesota obtained great value in the third round. Miller touted Tiernan as the potential "long-term plan at right tackle" considering starter Brian O'Neill will turn 31 years old in September; granted, O'Neill still is an exceptional player. Although Tiernan primarily played on the left side in college, Miller shared that scouts think he's equipped to man the right side or even switch to guard. At 43, Miller applauded the Vikings top choice of former Florida defensive lineman Caleb Banks. Miller wrote the following of the club's highest-drafted defensive lineman since Erasmus James in 2005: The biggest surprise of Round 1 — more than [quarterback Ty] Simpson (drafted 13th overall by the Rams) or [receiver Carnell] Tate (drafted 4th by the Titans) — was Banks finding his way into the top 20. Foot injuries sidelined Banks throughout last season and in the pre-draft process, but he should be seen as an immediate starter when healthy with Jonathan Allen and Javon Hargrave being released this offseason. The middle of the Vikings line was low on talent, and Banks is easily the most traits-rich player on the roster. Based on value, this pick is a big reach, but it fills a massive need. And the high-end potential from Banks makes it understandable in a draft class that was low on starting-level defensive tackles. Minnesota's other big addition to the defensive front, former Iowa State defensive tackle Domonique Orange, made the cut, too, popping up at 68 on Miller's list. Ironically, Orange was Miller's 68th prospect. Miller opined the Vikings were smart to circle back to their defensive line and take "Big Citrus," reasoning, "The return on investment will be huge if they can get two starting-level defensive tackles on rookie contracts." Like Banks, Miller added, Orange should have every opportunity to start in Week 1. At 83 and 85, Miller spotlighted Minnesota's new backfield tandem, award-winning former Michigan fullback Max Bredeson (Miller's 126th prospect, picked 159th) and speed-possessed former Wake Forest running back Demond Claiborne (Miller's 160th prospect, picked 198th). These choices are widely liked! Miller explained Bredeson was "invaluable to the Michigan offense" because of his ability to align in different spots. Miller elaborated with an obvious excitement: "He had no peers in the class as a blocker. If there's going to be a power run game in Minnesota, Bredeson is the fullback you want kicking it off." As for Claiborne, Miller sees him as "the lightning to the thunder of veteran Aaron Jones, Sr., and Bredeson," and he projects that Claiborne's pass-catching value on third down will be apparent quickly. Of course, Minnesota also has Jordan Mason, who played 16 games last season and had 159 carries for 758 rushing yards and six touchdowns. Miami is the only club with greater representation on Miller's list, garnering his stamp of approval six times: LB Jacob Rodriguez (Round 2-Pick 43 overall) at 14, WR Chris Bell (3-94) at 31, T Kaydyn Proctor (1-12) at 35, CB Chris Johnson (1-27) at 38, LB Kyle Louis (4-138) at 40 and edge rusher Max Llewellyn (7-237) at 93. Click here to read Miller's entire article and decide for yourself which franchises perhaps aced the draft. The Athletic's Alec Lewis supplied perspective Thursday about each of Minnesota's draft picks. With insights from college coaches and staffers, Lewis guided us from Banks, who former Florida head coach Billy Napier described as "a generational height, length, twitch and intelligence type of guy who also has a competitive streak that makes him different," to Day 3 center Gavin Gerhardt out of Cincinnati. You can read Lewis' full story here, and we will share some of the most interesting intel below. Starting in reverse order, Cincinnati's former director of player personnel Jack Griffith told Lewis that Gerhardt is "Probably the best servant leader I've ever been around," and added, "He could've left a few different times, but he was the linchpin who brought the cultures together between (Bearcats former head coach) Luke Fickell and (Head Coach) Scott Satterfield. He's the guy you trust to make all the calls." Maybe the best quote in Lewis' piece is attributed to Bill O'Boyle, who was Tiernan's offensive line coach in 2024 at Northwestern. Boyle mentioned Tiernan overcomes shorter arm length with hip mobility, and he listened closely to him teaching the Wildcats linemen how to use independent hands in pass blocking. Lewis stated that O'Boyle believes Tiernan's ceiling depends on opportunity. "We could put in a screen game where we could get him on the perimeter in a way you couldn't with other tackles," O'Boyle relayed to Lewis. "Because he's that fast. He's got that sneaky, fat-man speed." One other player worth mentioning is the fourth defender Minnesota picked in the Top 100 this year: safety Jakobe Thomas, who played under former Gophers defensive coordinator Corey Hetherman in the same role last season at Miami. Hetherman has studied Brian Flores' wizardry and identified overlap between what Thomas did for the Hurricanes and what he could potentially do for the Vikings. "His style of play, and their style of loading the box and doing different things with the safeties and being multiple, is ideal," Hetherman told Lewis. "He was also a guy who didn't come in until August (after playing at Middle Tennessee State), and he picked it up quickly. So, you'd think he'd do the same there." Canes safeties coach Will Harris, who helped coach Byron Murphy, Jr., at Washington, raved about Thomas' play style: "We always talked about enforcing your will on your opponent, and he did that." title: Becky G’s Corseted Nude Project Jacket Gets a Bold Micro Shorts Twist - AOL.com
content: Becky G delivered a sporty-meets-structured fashion moment during Coachella Weekend 2. The singer recently donned a coordinated set. Pairing it with a Nude Project jacket, her ensemble blended athletic elements with a cinched, corset-inspired silhouette. The singer wore a fitted blue Nude Project Challenger full-zip jacket, designed with a sculpted, corset-like structure that accentuated her waist while maintaining a sleek, performance-ready feel. She paired it with matching micro shorts from the same label, creating a cohesive set that balanced sharp tailoring with a laid-back festival vibe. Keeping the sporty aesthetic intact, Becky G completed the look with Adidas Ghost Sprint shoes in a metallic finish. The streamlined silhouette and monochrome palette allowed the textures and fit to stand out without overwhelming the overall styling. Spotted at a YouTube Backstage Studio setting during Coachella 2026, the look highlighted her ability to merge function with fashion. The structured jacket and minimal accessories kept the focus on clean lines and fit. Originally reported by Samridhi Goel on TheFashionSpot. The post Becky G’s Corseted Nude Project Jacket Gets a Bold Micro Shorts Twist appeared first on Mandatory.

title: 2026 GS Caltex Maekyung Open Golf Championship purse, winner's share, prize money payout - Golf News Net
content:
Golf News Net members don't see our floating video player (when logged in) and get an exclusive newsletter! By signing up for an account, you agree to sign up for our newsletter, The Break. You can unsubscribe at any time and keep your membership. The 2026 GS Caltex Maekyung Open Golf Championship purse is set for ₩1,300,000,000 ($880,409), with the winner's share coming in at ₩234,000,000 ($158,473) -- the standard 18 percent payout according to the Asian Tour's prize money distribution chart. The GS Caltex Maekyung Open Golf Championship field is headed by Jeunghun Wang, Jbe Kruger, Chase Koepka and more of the Asian Tour's best players. The 144-player field is the sixth event on the Asian Tour schedule and is the latest event of the 2026 season. A cut is made after 36 holes to the top 65 players and ties for the final two rounds of the tournament. All players who finish four rounds of the tournament will earn money. The event is played this year at Namseoul Country Club in South Korea. Beyond money, there are important points, perks and benefits on the line for the field -- in particular, the tournament winner. The winner of this event will get add to their Order of Merit tally, which is crucial for access to tournaments and bonuses. The winner gets 8.9 Official World Golf Ranking points, with the points available based on field strength. Winning comes with other big benefits, including a two-plus season exemption on the Asian Tour. Ryan Ballengee is founder and editor of Golf News Net. He has been writing and broadcasting about golf for nearly 20 years. Ballengee lives in the Washington, D.C. area with his family. title: Logitech G’s Racing Wheel for PlayStation and Xbox Gets Multiple Price Cuts on Amazon, Perfect Driving Game Set-Up for Less - Kotaku
content:
This article is part of Kotaku Deals, produced separately from the editorial team.
      We may earn a commission when you buy through links on the site. If you’re a huge racing game fan and you’ve been looking for a way to upgrade your gaming set-up, but haven’t found the right way to do it, then we’ve got the perfect deal for you. After all, what could be better than investing in an amazing racing wheel and then really locking-in whether you’re trying to take pole position, come out on top in Forza, or you’re just really into kart racers. While there are a lot of good wheels out there, the Logitech G RS50 Racing Wheel is one of the best, which is why you’d normally have to pay $700 for it. That’s a lot of money, but today on Amazon it’s on sale with a 25% discount, which brings the price down to $525 instead. That’s still a lot, but this thing is top-of-the-line for a reason. Both the PlayStation and Xbox variants are at the same price today, although if you’re looking for one of these for your gaming PC, then you can actually grab either one with no issues. This thing boasts some seriously impressive specs, and delivers Direct Drive force feedback thanks to TrueForce, which means that you’ll feel the wheel fighting you as it would in an actual race, and it has a max torque of up to 8 Nm. Not only that, but with a 2,700-degree max rotation, you can really go for it on those corners for a level of realism and immersion that most other wheels just can’t compare too. The wheel itself is 11-inches, and has 13 buttons along with adjustable paddle shifters, once again helping you to feel like you’re really in the car. All you need to do is attach it to a surface and then sit back and get driving, and you’ll never look back. You can even customize it further if you want to spend a bit more money, with a few of the parts being easy to swap out for other Logitch Racing pieces, meaning that this is something that can be what you want, when you want, and it’ll grow with you as get better at using it. This is an incredible deal, but it’s also one that’s not likely to last for very long. If you can afford it while it’s down to $525, then this is the perfect chance to buy it, because there’s no way of knowing when it’ll be on sale again, and missing out this time could sting. title: BMW R1300 GS Adventure Dark Edition revealed - Visordown
content: BMW Motorrad has revealed a new R1300 GS Adventure Dark Edition, but sadly, it’s only available for French customers. BMW Motorrad France has done it again, this time unveiling a new R1300 GS Adventure Dark Edition to go along with the recent special edition F900 R and F900 XR models. The F900 R and F900 XR special edition models received Frozen Grey (F900 R), and Aurelius Green (F900 XR) liveries, with both motorcycles using the German company's car division for inspiration. Like those two models, the new R1300 GS Adventure Dark Edition is for the French market only, which was also the case regarding the special edition F900 GS and R1300 GS models that were revealed last year. The R1300 GS Adventure Dark Edition will be sold in France for €22,900, which at current exchange rates converts to around £20,000. That also makes it €1,700 (around £1,470) more than the standard R1300 GS Adventure model in France. The new special edition variant will be available to buy until 30 June. It includes a Triple Black style with black spoked wheels, heated rider and passenger seats, a high tinted windshield, tinted deflectors, black-painted scoops on the tank, and an alarm. Just like the standard model, the R1300 GS Adventure Dark Edition gets Dynamic Cruise Control as standard, as well as other features such as Dynamic Traction Control, ABS, four riding modes (Eco, Rain, Road, Enduro), heated grips, and additional LED spotlights. Alongside the R1300 GS, the standard Adventure model is currently one of BMW’s two most popular motorcycles. In terms of total sales in 2025, the R1300 GS Adventure was the top-selling model with 33,570 units sold, compared to the R1300 GS’ 32,555. Both models were also very popular in the UK throughout 2025, although it was the GSA that spent most months at the top of the adventure sales charts. The GSA was the UK’s best-seller in seven out of 12 months last year. title: Gen.G's 'Solid Top Side' Leads to 2-1 Victory Over Resilient Kiwoom DRX - Inven Global
content: In the 2026 League of Legends Champions Korea (LCK) Spring Split match held at LoL Park in Jonggak on the 30th, Gen.G Esports defeated Kiwoom DRX 2-1. While Kiwoom DRX put up a valiant fight, the series ultimately showcased the superior individual skill and 'weight class' of Gen.G's top side. Gen.G dominated from the first set. Their mid and bot lanes pulled ahead during the laning phase, and the bot lane's balance completely collapsed following intervention from 'Canyon' Kim Geon-bu's Jarvan IV. Leveraging their lead, Gen.G monopolized objectives and snowballed the game. 'Chovy' Jeong Ji-hoon's Annie quickly racked up six kills, and Gen.G took the first set with a 10,000-gold lead. Kiwoom DRX started the second set on a high note, with 'Willer' Kim Jeong-hyeon's Wukong securing consecutive kills in the mid and bot lanes. The Wukong grew to carry-level strength, and despite a brief response from Gen.G's bot duo, the Wukong remained a constant threat. Turning their attention to the top lane, Kiwoom DRX repeatedly shut down 'Kiin' Kim Gi-in's Jayce and picked up additional kills in skirmishes around the top side. Gen.G, however, was not a team to fold easily. Trailing by over 4,000 gold, they executed a brilliant team fight to secure a massive win and take Baron. Although Kiwoom DRX maintained the lead, they were pushed to the brink when 'Chovy's' Akali picked off their bot duo. Yet, around the 29-minute mark, a team fight broke out before Akali could join; Kiwoom DRX successfully focused down 'Ruler' Park Jae-hyuk's Jhin, secured a decisive victory, and took the second set. In the deciding third set, 'Kiin's' K'Sante dominated 'Rich's' Shen in the laning phase, effectively neutralizing Kiwoom DRX's strategy centered around the Shen. Without even securing a kill, the gold gap between the top laners widened to nearly 1,400. To Kiwoom DRX, the fed K'Sante was an immovable mountain. Even when they managed to catch out an opponent to initiate a fight, they lost consecutive skirmishes due to the sheer presence of the K'Sante, who refused to go down. Around the 25-minute mark, Kiwoom DRX finally took down the troublesome K'Sante. They claimed the Dragon as a reward and finished off 'Ruler's' Jhin, who was caught in an overextended position. However, Gen.G responded by picking off 'Andil' Moon Gwan-bin's Nautilus and securing Baron, further widening the gap. Kiwoom DRX tried to hold on, banking on the late-game carry potential of 'Ucal' Son Woo-hyeon's Viktor, but it was an uphill battle with both nexus turrets already exposed. Ultimately, Gen.G secured the third set victory through a backdoor play by 'Chovy'. title: Titan at Caltech, forgotten in India: The science genius who made architecture cool - India Today
content: In the grand story of modern engineering, some names echo loudly while others quietly shape the world from behind the scenes. Guruvayur Subramanian Ramaswamy belongs firmly to the latter. A pioneer who fused physics with architecture, Ramaswamy redefined how large structures are built, yet remains largely unknown outside academic circles in India. Born in 1923, Ramaswamy approached buildings not as stacks of bricks or steel, but as systems governed by invisible forces. His intellectual journey took him to California Institute of Technology (Caltech) during its golden era, where he trained under some of the world’s leading minds in structural and fluid mechanics. It was here that he began to see design through the lens of physics, especially the power of curvature. Inspired by something as simple as an eggshell, Ramaswamy asked a radical question: why do we rely on heavy materials when nature achieves strength through shape? His answer transformed engineering. He pioneered the concept of funicular shell structures, using principles like the catenary curve, the natural shape formed by a hanging chain, to design roofs that were astonishingly thin yet incredibly strong. A funicular shell structure is a thin, curved roof or surface designed so that forces flow naturally through it, mainly as compression. Inspired by shapes like a hanging chain turned upside down, it uses minimal material yet remains very strong because the shape carries the load efficiently rather than relying on thickness. Instead of fighting gravity with bulk, Ramaswamy’s designs worked with it. By aligning structures along the natural flow of forces, he proved that strength could come from geometry rather than sheer mass. The result: vast industrial roofs, domes, and hangars that used far less material without compromising stability. Back in India, he became the founding director of the Structural Engineering Research Centre, helping establish the country as a global leader in shell structures decades before such ideas became mainstream in the West. His work enabled cost-effective construction, a critical need for developing nations. Yet, Ramaswamy occupied an unusual intellectual space. To engineers, his work seemed too theoretical; to physicists, too applied. This made him a ghost figure, influential, but not widely celebrated. His seminal book, Design and Construction of Concrete Shell Roofs, became a global reference text, shaping projects from the United States to Russia. He was awarded the Shanti Swarup Bhatnagar Prize, one of India’s highest scientific honours, and served as a consultant to the United Nations, advising countries on low-cost, high-efficiency construction. Yet his recognition largely remained confined to professional circles. Next in who after the Bibha Series? He used the math of an eggshell to build the roofs of the future. Dr. Guruvayur Subramanian Ramaswamy (1923-2002) was the Ghost who decoded the physics of curvature. While the world was using heavy steel & thick concrete, he was using Pure pic.twitter.com/EeYR7CgFVI— Parimal (@Fintech03) April 30, 2026 To his family, he was a disciplined academic. To the world, he was a quiet revolutionary. Today, the sweeping roofs of factories, warehouses, and public spaces, structures that seem to float effortlessly, owe much to his ideas. And yet, there are no statues, no public memory, no widespread recognition of his name in India. Ramaswamy’s legacy is not carved in stone, but embedded in it. Every thin shell that stands strong against gravity is a testament to his belief: that the smartest structures are not the heaviest, but the most intelligent.

title: Renowned advocates GS Ghuman and GPS Ghuman joined AAP, welcomed by CM Mann - Punjab Newsline
content: Punjab Newsline live updates • Latest Punjab headlines • Video and photo stories updated hourly • Stay connected with regional and national coverage • Punjab Newsline live updates • Latest Punjab headlines Legal fraternity’s “Ghuman Brothers” strengthen Aam Aadmi Party as leadership highlights rising public trust Prominent advocates GS Ghuman and GPS Ghuman joined the Aam Aadmi Party in Punjab, with Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann highlighting growing public trust in the party. Their entry is expected to strengthen AAP’s legal and regional influence. Punjab Newsline | ChandigarhImpressed by the policies of the Aam Aadmi Party and its pro-people initiatives, the trend of prominent individuals joining the party continues to gain momentum in Punjab. On Thursday, well-known advocates GS Ghuman and GPS Ghuman formally joined AAP. Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann welcomed the duo into the party and administered them membership. He stated that the inclusion of respected and intellectual figures from society reflects the increasing faith of the public in the government’s honest and transparent politics. Popularly known in legal circles as the “Ghuman Brothers,” the duo enjoys a strong reputation not only in the legal fraternity but also across various sections of society. Their induction is expected to bolster the party’s legal expertise and enhance its presence, particularly in the Majha and Malwa regions. After joining AAP, the Ghuman Brothers expressed admiration for the leadership and governance style of Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann. They pledged to work with full dedication for Punjab’s development and to amplify the voice of the common people. Pioneering regional digital journalism since 2005. title: The biggest point to watch at the 45th GS Caltex Maekyung Open, "Korea's Masters," is whether or not.. - 매일경제
content:
The biggest point to watch at the 45th GS Caltex Maekyung Open, "Korea's Masters," is whether or not foreign national players will win. Since Mark Calcavecchia (USA) in 2004, no foreign national has won the championship. Recently, Thai players have been challenged hard at the GS Caltex Maekyung Open. Thai players, including Chonrati Tengbun Ngam at the 43rd event in 2024, and Jazz Zsenwatanon at the 44th event in 2025, were runner-up in succession. With 16 Thai players challenging this year, Pawit Ttangmolprasut, ranked third in the Order of Merit category, which is the point of the Asian Tour, announced a smooth start to conquer the South Seoul CC. In the first round of the tournament held at Nam Seoul CC in Seongnam, Gyeonggi Province on the 30th, Prasut recorded 5-under 66, tying for second, one stroke behind the lone leader Lee Tae-hee (6-under 65). Jointly ranked 33rd in the 2024 competition was his best performance at the GS Caltex Maekyung Open, but he started the competition in a good mood by reducing five strokes while going back and forth in the cold and hot bath on the first day of this competition. "I'm very happy with my game," said Chirasut after the game. Everything was smooth and the flow was good. A bit of a fancy scorecard came out, but I kept my pace well. I'm very satisfied with my performance," he said. On this day, he had an eagle at the par-5 fourth hole, and while he had seven birdies, he wrote down four bogeys. "Honestly, I haven't played a lot of good games at South Seoul CC," he said. The course is also difficult. But I tried to play happily," he said. "One mistake can lead to a score. I made a few mistakes, but I didn't care and focused only on the next shot," he said. Trunk Prasut, who has two wins in the Asian Tour, is showing the most stable performance among Thai players in this season's Asian Tour. He made it through all four Asian Tour tournaments this season, including the Philippine Golf Championship in February and the International Series Japan in April. Regarding his recent good performance, he cited "strong mentality" as his secret. "After the first four tournaments, I gained a lot of confidence in my game. I think it all depends on the mentality," he said. Another reason for the rise was the changed training routine. "The last few years have been a long vacation," he said. This year, he continued to train and participate in the competition without a break due to preparations for the LIV Golf Promotion. In the meantime, he said, "We have been consistently performing well." ※ This article was translated using AI technology for reader convenience. title: GS E&C Housing Revenue Falls Below 2 Trillion Won; Operating Profit Up 4.4% - Seoul Economic Daily
content:
Operating Profit Reaches 73.5 Billion Won
Revenue Falls 21.6% to 2.4 Trillion Won
Urban Redevelopment Orders Exceed 4 Trillion Won This Year GS E&C (006360.KS) reported first-quarter operating profit of 73.5 billion won ($54 million), up 4.4% from a year earlier, the company said in a regulatory filing. Revenue fell 21.6% to 2.4 trillion won ($1.77 billion) over the same period. By business division, the architecture and housing division's revenue fell below the 2 trillion won mark to 1.42 trillion won, down 29.3% from 2.01 trillion won a year earlier. The plant division posted 253.6 billion won in revenue, while the infrastructure division recorded 326.4 billion won. The company said revenue at the architecture and housing division declined as housing supply shrank amid the recent downturn in the construction and real estate market, but it expects revenue to rebound once full-scale construction begins on projects for which about 10,000 units are scheduled to be supplied through the first half of this year. First-quarter new orders reached 2.6 trillion won. The architecture and housing division secured orders including the Osan Yangsan District 4 Apartment Project (497.1 billion won) and the Geoyeo Saemaeul Housing Redevelopment Project (326.3 billion won), while Danwood, the company's Poland-based modular construction subsidiary, won 119.1 billion won in new orders. With contractor selections expected this year at several urban redevelopment sites in major areas, including along Seoul's Han River, GS E&C was recently selected as the contractor for the 2.15 trillion won Seongsu Strategic Redevelopment Zone District 1 Housing Redevelopment Project, leveraging its Xi brand. The company has secured more than 4 trillion won in urban redevelopment contracts so far this year, and new orders are expected to increase further. "Amid persistent uncertainty in the external and internal environment, we will focus on securing profitability through sound management and identifying future growth drivers, building on our long-accumulated capabilities and successful business experience to strengthen the foundation for sustainable management," a GS E&C official said. title: 500 free gelato cups at Tofu G’s new outlet - Yahoo Lifestyle Singapore
content: Korean dessert brand Tofu G is expanding into Singapore’s CBD with a new outlet at UE Square, ready to delight with its signature tofu-based gelato. If you are in the area on 1 May 2026, there is a good reason to swing by early. To celebrate the grand opening, the brand is treating customers to 500 free cups of its newest creation, Pomegranate Tofu Gelato. It is a 1-day, first-come, first-served event, with each person limited to 1 cup. The new Pomegranate Tofu Gelato is the star of the launch. It has a fruit-forward profile, balancing sweetness with a slight tarty edge for a refreshing taste. It works well with the tofu base, cutting through the creaminess and leaving a clean finish. If you prefer something more familiar, the menu still includes staples like Vegan Tofu Gelato, Vegan Rich Chocolate Gelato, Vegan Black Sesame Gelato and Pistachio Gelato, all priced at S$8 per scoop. The concept draws on Korean tofu-making techniques, giving the brand a distinct identity in a dessert scene packed with options. It is entirely vegan, emphasising high-protein, low-fat ingredients. The gelato menu leans lighter on the palate, making it an easy pick for those who want dessert without the heaviness of traditional gelato. Everything is made in-store, giving each batch a fresher taste and a consistency that feels carefully crafted rather than mass-produced. If you miss the opening day giveaway, there is still a reason to return. From 2 to 8 May 2026, customers who purchase a Pomegranate Tofu Gelato can enjoy a free double scoop in another flavour. This simple deal makes it easier to sample more of the brand’s offerings in 1 visit. With its central location and a concept that feels different from usual dessert spots, Tofu G’s newest outlet is well-positioned for quick office breaks and after-dinner treats. If you are curious about tofu-based desserts, this might be the place to start. For a full list of locations, opening hours & their contact number, refer here. title: 13 Adventure Bikes Owners Rarely Trade In - Top Speed
content: We all want a forever bike, the kind that we fall in love with and don’t ever want to give up. It doesn't matter whether it happens at first sight or over a period of time. Adventure bikes have a very high chance of becoming bikes forever because they have such a broad range of abilities, and they can be fun for everything from the commute to a weekend in the dirt. They are also over-engineered, which means they are built to last. That is a killer combination of reliability, fun, and simply a great ownership experience. Here are ten adventure bikes that are so good at what they do that their owners rarely trade them in. We have updated the article with three more entries. The CRF300L Rally is a little mountain goat of an adventure bike because it is based on a dual-sport. Additionally, the only real changes it has are cosmetic, other than a larger fuel tank and a bigger front brake disc. Given what it is, it will also be around for a very long time because it hangs its hat on lightness and reliability rather than a high power figure or great performance. It has minimal electronics and an LCD. The rear ABS is switchable, and this is light enough for you to take fearlessly where no adventure bike has gone before. The Himalayan 450 is an adventure bike in the truest sense of the word, in that it can tour long distances without becoming too uncomfortable. And when you ask it to take you off-road, it will do it happily. A lot of this motorcycle is a first for Royal Enfield, which explains the quirks like the guard-mounted front indicators, the lack of a traditional tail lamp, and the necessity to keep your phone screen on when you are using navigation on the TFT display, unless you have an Android phone. For the sub-$7,000 price, this is a lot of motorcycle. The Honda NX500 has fallen off the radar in view of its much more capable new rivals, yet it remains a dependable choice in the segment. This ADV isn’t anything special; on the contrary, it offers an easy-going character with a smooth parallel-twin engine and nimble handling. And it doesn’t handle off-roading as well as more dirt-ready rivals, but it’s the kind of bike that will never leave you stranded. If you had to pick the most reliable and approachable ADV, the NX500 is the one to get. The KTM 390 Adventure R is possibly the most feature-rich and off-road capable small ADV in the market right now. It has KTM's off-road DNA in it, but KTM has not pulled any punches and equipped it with a by-wire throttle, a six-axis IMU, and a TFT instrument cluster. An assist and slipper clutch and two-way quickshifter are included, alongside fully-adjustable long-travel suspension and ABS that can be switched off at the rear. The instrument cluster also has turn-by-turn navigation as standard, and the spoke wheels have tubed, aggressive off-road tires. From Honda to KTM, these dual-sport bikes are ready to tackle any terrain. The KLR650 might have started out life as a dual-sport, but with added features came weight. Kawasaki then leaned into it completely and gave us a variant called the KLR650 Adventure ABS. It isn't fast, it lacks features, and it isn't refined. What it is is indestructible. This is the motorcycle you take when you want to ride around the world and don't have a time limit. It can be repaired pretty much anywhere despite its latest advances in tech, namely fuel injection and ABS. And the awesome aftermarket support will allow you to customize it any way you like, ensuring you never get bored with it. The V-Strom 650XT is quite like the KLR650 Adventure in that it has not changed for a very long time, but it does not want to either. It knows what it is, and it fills a niche so well that there is no real need to change it. It is fun when you push it, it is as reliable as anything you might encounter, and its mechanical simplicity only adds to its appeal. Change might be on the cards in the near future, however, thanks to emissions norms. The Transalp has always been one of the value picks in the middleweight adventure bike segment. The price has crept to over $10,000 now, though. The reason is that the 2026 model has additional features. The headlining one is the E-Clutch system that allows you to operate it like a semi-automatic gearbox. You can switch it off when you want to use the clutch lever off-road. Other additions include fully-adjustable suspension at both ends, and a standard aluminum engine bash plate. This remains the best value proposition in its segment even now. These touring bikes have it all, comfort as well as clearance The Tenere 700 remains the best middleweight adventure bike for off-road purposes. Yamaha has now smoothed out some of its rough edges by adding a by-wire throttle, a taller windscreen, and an optional two-way quickshifter. It still lacks a six-axis IMU, but if you want that, you can always spend a little more money on the Tenere 700 World Raid (shown below), which is now available on the US market. Either way, you cannot go wrong, and you will get something that is both very enjoyable to ride and very serious about playing in the dirt. We have called the Africa Twin the ultimate adventure motorcycle with good reason. It is reasonably priced, is immensely reliable, and is perfectly willing to do either long miles on tarmac or head across the desert like an arrow to the horizon. In the real world, it has more than enough performance to keep a smile on your face. It is not short on tech either; it has the segment's only true automatic gearbox, a dual-clutch one. If you opt for the Adventure Sports, you can have electronic suspension, too. And that is before we talk about the large touchscreen TFT with wireless Android Auto and Apple CarPlay. . The TL1000S may have earned a reputation for being a widowmaker, but its silver lining was its impressive V-twin engine. It was known for being nearly indestructible, and today, that engine lives on in the Suzuki V-Strom 1050. This engine can rack up some serious mileage; we’ve even found one DL1000 K3 (V-Strom 1050’s predecessor) with 400k miles on it. On this bike, the engine is paired with modern features and enough capability to take you around the world, especially in its 1050DR Adventure avatar. Found an error? Send it info@www.topspeed.com so it can be corrected. We want to hear from you. Share your perspective in the comments below, and please keep the conversation respectful. I just bought a leftover 2025 model.  It has the quick shift, which is as good as any e-clutch I could imagine.  Got a fair deal. $0 down 36 months and the payment is low. Shockingly low.  I am more impressed every time i ride it.

title: GS Group Provides Free AI Tools to SMEs, Bridging AI Gap - 조선일보
content: GS's AI agent AIR and MISO platform, developed with on-site expertise, now used by 130+ SMEs GS Group is actively building an "AI coexistence ecosystem" by providing its self-developed AI platform and agents to small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) at no cost. The conglomerate aims to bridge the AI gap between large corporations and smaller businesses by sharing its AI transformation (AX) expertise, honed over years of on-site experience. GS is pursuing this openness based on the strategic judgment that when SMEs adopt AI, a virtuous cycle of accumulating on-site data and advancing technological sophistication will emerge. Beyond merely enhancing its corporate image, the company believes that elevating the overall AI competitiveness of the industrial ecosystem will ultimately benefit GS in the long term. **◇AI Agent Developed by On-Site Employees, Provided Free to 130 SMEs** The safety management AI agent "AIR (Air)," which GS Group is distributing to SMEs free of charge, was conceived and developed by GS Power’s on-site employees. At the "3rd GS Group Hackathon" in 2024, five safety and machinery field practitioners proposed the idea and developed it using GS’s proprietary AX platform "MISO (MISO)" without coding. By simply inputting a task name and brief description, the generative AI automatically generates work processes, potential risk factors, risk levels, and preventive measures. Designed for small workplaces lacking safety management personnel and expertise, the solution enables systematic safety management. GS Power integrated AIR into its internal systems and refined the solution based on on-site data. As a result, the time required for risk assessments—previously conducted manually by cross-referencing the Occupational Safety and Health Act and manuals—was reduced to one-tenth of the original duration. Uniform evaluation quality is now maintained regardless of the assessor’s skill level, allowing more time to be allocated to actual on-site safety inspections rather than repetitive documentation. Over 130 small business sites currently utilize AIR, and GS Group plans to expand its distribution further. A GS Group official stated, "AIR is an AX case that prioritizes the field over technology. We hope SMEs will directly experience the benefits of AI-based safety management and that this will substantially reduce safety gaps in industrial sites." This initiative aligns with Chairman Huh Tae-soo’s emphasis on "AI coexistence." He has consistently stressed, "In the digital era, fostering an ecosystem—not going solo—is essential for business." He has encouraged actively sharing GS’s AI assets to support mid-sized and small companies struggling to secure talent. **◇‘MISO’ Platform with 10,000 Tools, Transferring Know-How to SMEs** The platform "MISO," which gave birth to AIR, is a core asset of GS’s coexistence strategy. MISO allows non-technical employees to implement webpages or business tools simply by conversing with the system. It is designed to securely update company data and freely apply AI models optimized for on-site conditions. Currently, GS employees have developed over 10,000 tools using MISO, with 140 of them integrated into affiliate systems and utilized across production, logistics, and customer data analysis. By sharing MISO’s know-how with SMEs that lack IT infrastructure, GS aims to lower the entry barrier to AI transformation regardless of company size. GS Group’s achievements have also been recognized externally. In January of this year, GS Power was selected as an "Outstanding Case for Spreading Process Safety Management (PSM) Safety Culture" and received the Minister of Employment and Labor Award. The company was highly evaluated not only for enhancing the efficiency and quality of safety management through AI but also for its efforts to expand these solutions to external workplaces. A GS official remarked, "Cases where large corporations open their field-verified AI assets are still rare. GS’s approach could become a new model for AI coexistence between large and small businesses."

title: Hutchinson bets on gravel racing with Touareg Race, Caracal Race GS, and tires up to 50 mm - Brujulabike.com
content: The French company Hutchinson has just launched a deep update of its gravel tire range. It introduces new models, technologies inherited from road cycling, and a clear focus on modern competition. The launch revolves around three tires: Touareg Race, Touareg Race GS, and Caracal Race GS, with a very defined focus on efficiency, control, and adaptation to different racing scenarios. Competitive gravel has evolved into a terrain where data, energy efficiency, and details make real differences, and Hutchinson proposes a solution based on combining tires instead of seeking a single tire for everything. The development of the new Touareg Race starts from a known base but incorporates key elements from the brand's fastest model, the Hutchinson Caracal Race. The goal is to maintain the versatility of the Touareg tread while reducing energy losses and improving pure performance. The use of the Mach Tread 3.0 compound introduces measurable changes in dynamic behavior. According to the provided data, it increases rebound capacity by 25% and tear resistance by 10%, which translates to less energy dissipation and greater durability in aggressive conditions. This is complemented by the SwiftEasy casing, which reduces weight and improves controlled tire deformation, a key factor in maintaining stability without penalizing rolling resistance. The result is a tire that starts at 480 grams and, according to the brand, improves efficiency by 35% compared to the previous Hardskin version. In practical terms, this means a more finely tuned balance between three critical variables in competitive gravel: rolling resistance, structural control of the casing, and grip in fast corners. Hutchinson also proposes a clear usage configuration. The Touareg Race is positioned as a front tire to maximize control and precision, combined with a Hutchinson Caracal Race rear tire focused on speed. According to the brand itself, this combination covers approximately 90% of the usual scenarios in gravel races. This tire is available in 40, 45, and 50 mm, and the last size reflects an increasingly established trend in the segment. The other major novelty is the arrival of GridSkin technology in gravel. It consists of a reinforcement layer in the form of a woven mesh that specifically acts on the tire's sidewalls, a critical area in rocky or aggressive conditions. Hutchinson Touareg Race GS and Hutchinson Caracal Race GS incorporate this solution, which aims to isolate cuts and tears without adding significant penalties in weight or rolling resistance. The approach is clear: to offer a more robust option for races where reliability is as crucial as speed. This type of reinforcement responds to a reality of current gravel, where many events combine fast sections with technical areas that push the integrity of the tire to the limit. Another relevant move is the complete expansion to 50 mm sections across the entire range. This is not limited to the Touareg Race but also includes the Caracal Race, which will be available in this width for the first time. This decision is directly connected to the evolution of materials in gravel. Wider rims and lower pressures are pushing towards tires with greater volume, which improve comfort, traction, and efficiency on uneven terrain. The entire range maintains tubeless ready construction and compatibility with modern wider internal rims, a standard already established in competition. With this launch, Hutchinson not only expands its catalog but also redefines its approach to competitive gravel, betting on a modular system based on tire combinations and adaptation to the terrain. For the brand, the key is not in a single universal model but in optimizing each setup according to the actual racing conditions. Subscribe to our newsletter and receive all our news. Shares of this investment bank have returned +9.5% over the past month versus the Zacks S&P 500 composite's +12.2% change. The Zacks Financial - Investment Bank industry, to which Goldman belongs, has gained 12% over this period. Now the key question is: Where could the stock be headed in the near term? While media releases or rumors about a substantial change in a company's business prospects usually make its stock 'trending' and lead to an immediate price change, there are always some fundamental facts that eventually dominate the buy-and-hold decision-making. Here at Zacks, we prioritize appraising the change in the projection of a company's future earnings over anything else. That's because we believe the present value of its future stream of earnings is what determines the fair value for its stock. We essentially look at how sell-side analysts covering the stock are revising their earnings estimates to reflect the impact of the latest business trends. And if earnings estimates go up for a company, the fair value for its stock goes up. A higher fair value than the current market price drives investors' interest in buying the stock, leading to its price moving higher. This is why empirical research shows a strong correlation between trends in earnings estimate revisions and near-term stock price movements. Goldman is expected to post earnings of $13.71 per share for the current quarter, representing a year-over-year change of +25.7%. Over the last 30 days, the Zacks Consensus Estimate has changed -2.5%. For the current fiscal year, the consensus earnings estimate of $59.53 points to a change of +16% from the prior year. Over the last 30 days, this estimate has changed +3.5%. For the next fiscal year, the consensus earnings estimate of $65.79 indicates a change of +10.5% from what Goldman is expected to report a year ago. Over the past month, the estimate has changed +4.4%. Having a strong externally audited track record, our proprietary stock rating tool, the Zacks Rank, offers a more conclusive picture of a stock's price direction in the near term, since it effectively harnesses the power of earnings estimate revisions. Due to the size of the recent change in the consensus estimate, along with three other factors related to earnings estimates, Goldman is rated Zacks Rank #3 (Hold). The chart below shows the evolution of the company's forward 12-month consensus EPS estimate: While earnings growth is arguably the most superior indicator of a company's financial health, nothing happens as such if a business isn't able to grow its revenues. After all, it's nearly impossible for a company to increase its earnings for an extended period without increasing its revenues. So, it's important to know a company's potential revenue growth. In the case of Goldman, the consensus sales estimate of $15.75 billion for the current quarter points to a year-over-year change of +8%. The $63.7 billion and $66.81 billion estimates for the current and next fiscal years indicate changes of +9.3% and +4.9%, respectively. Goldman reported revenues of $17.23 billion in the last reported quarter, representing a year-over-year change of +14.4%. EPS of $17.55 for the same period compares with $14.12 a year ago. Compared to the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $16.98 billion, the reported revenues represent a surprise of +1.48%. The EPS surprise was +7.41%. The company beat consensus EPS estimates in each of the trailing four quarters. The company topped consensus revenue estimates three times over this period. Without considering a stock's valuation, no investment decision can be efficient. In predicting a stock's future price performance, it's crucial to determine whether its current price correctly reflects the intrinsic value of the underlying business and the company's growth prospects. While comparing the current values of a company's valuation multiples, such as price-to-earnings (P/E), price-to-sales (P/S), and price-to-cash flow (P/CF), with its own historical values helps determine whether its stock is fairly valued, overvalued, or undervalued, comparing the company relative to its peers on these parameters gives a good sense of the reasonability of the stock's price. As part of the Zacks Style Scores system, the Zacks Value Style Score (which evaluates both traditional and unconventional valuation metrics) organizes stocks into five groups ranging from A to F (A is better than B; B is better than C; and so on), making it helpful in identifying whether a stock is overvalued, rightly valued, or temporarily undervalued. title: WJT Returns to Dushanbe with Unmissable GS Action - IJF
content: Nestled in Central Asia, Tajikistan is a country of dramatic mountains, rich history and a deep-rooted sporting culture. Judo holds a special place in society, with growing popularity and strong institutional support. In recent years, the nation has made significant strides on the international stage, highlighted by its two Olympic judo medals at the Paris 2024 Olympic Games. In the French capital, Tajik athletes secured two bronze medals in a landmark moment for the nation. The country had already won an Olympic bronze medal in 2008 at the Beijing Games. Those achievements have accelerated investment in infrastructure and athlete development, transforming the country into a rising force in global judo. At the heart of this movement lies Dushanbe, a vibrant and rapidly developing capital. Known for its wide boulevards and welcoming atmosphere, Dushanbe has become a recognised hub for international judo events. The Kasri Tennis arena, which has hosted competitions for several years, will once again provide the stage for world-class action. With only two events remaining before the start of Olympic qualification, in Mongolia next month, the Dushanbe Grand Slam promises to deliver high stakes and high intensity. For athletes, every contest matters. For fans, it is an opportunity to witness some of the finest judoka in the world competing with full commitment. And if there is one thing that defines Dushanbe, it is its atmosphere. The Tajik crowd is among the most passionate and energetic on the circuit. Long before the first contest begins, spectators gather in and around the arena, creating a unique buzz that builds throughout the day. Once the action begins, the energy is constant, every ippon celebrated, every effort recognised. For the 2026 edition, nearly 250 athletes from 34 nations are expected to compete. The diversity of participants ensures a rich and unpredictable competition, where emerging talent meets established stars. If you are looking for spectacular judo, you will find it. If you are looking for ippon, you will see it, and if you are looking for an unforgettable atmosphere, Dushanbe will deliver. All the ingredients are in place for a memorable event. title: Pelicans NBA Draft Prospect Profile: Illinois GS Andrej Stojakovic - roundtable.io
content: The New Orleans Pelicans have a great talent evaluator in Troy Weaver and an aggressive leader in Joe Dumars, so they should never be counted out to grab a player that they like. They have a second-round pick from the Detroit Pistons to work with in this year's draft, but have not ruled out the possibility of going higher. Right now, though, the focus should be on the players who will be available in the second round, whom the Pelicans could consider. Continuing to go through second-round options, one player who makes sense is Illinois Fighting Illini guard Andrej Stojakovic. If that last name sounds familiar, it's for a reason. His father is 13-year NBA veteran, former New Orleans Hornets standout Peja Stojakovic. The older Stojakovic was a sharpshooter that was ahead of his time in the league. He spent five years in New Orleans, averaging 14.3 points, 4.0 rebounds and 1.3 assists per game on 41.8/40.4/89.9 shooting splits. The younger Stojakovic has declared for the draft, which gives him a chance to get feedback from NBA teams. He could still end up going back, though, if he doesn't like what he's hearing. He will always have fans around the league as an NBA legacy player, but he isn't a surefire thing right now. He has already said that he'd return to play next season, so it's really unclear if he'll actually get drafted. If Andrej does head to the league, the Pelicans would make perfect sense as a fit. Not only because his father played in the city while he was in the league, but they need more scoring from the wing. Stojakovic has spent time at three different colleges throughout his career. He started with the Stanford Cardinal, where he was mostly just a bench piece. He then transferred to the Cal Golden Bears as a sophomore, where he was the centerpiece of that offense. It didn't lead to a lot of winning, but he did well individually. He averaged 17.9 points, 4.7 rebounds, 1.8 assists and 1.2 blocks per game on 42.7/31.8/81.8 shooting splits. The 21-year-old then took a smaller role for more winning with the Illini this past season. He put up 13.5 points, 4.5 rebounds, 1.0 assists on 50.0/24.4/80.5 shooting splits. He's a hard-nosed defender and projects as a valuable defensive player as long as the effort translates. On offense, he doesn't really create much for others, but he has potential to be a three-level scorer.

title: Assessing Goldman Sachs (GS) Valuation After Strong 1 Year Shareholder Returns - simplywall.st

Goldman Sachs Group (GS) has been drawing attention after recent share price moves, with the stock last closing at US$926.55 and showing mixed returns over the past month and past 3 months. Despite a 1.2% single day share price decline, the recent 15.4% 1 month share price return and strong 1 year total shareholder return of 72.29% suggest momentum has generally been building over time. Goldman Sachs Group's most followed narrative pegs fair value at about $934 per share, slightly above the last close at $926.55, which implies only a modest valuation gap and focuses attention on the earnings engine behind that number. title: Karol G’s “Tropitour” World Tour: How to Get Tickets - Consequence of Sound
content:
Fresh off headlining Coachella, the Colombian superstar will return to stadiums in North America this summer and fall Karol G has announced a massive new world stadium tour. Officially titled the “Viajando Por El Mundo Tropitour,” the 40-plus-date run will take the Colombian pop singer across North America, Latin America, and Europe over the next year and a half. The tour follows her historic headlining set at Coachella 2026, where she became the first Latina artist to top the festival’s bill. The run commences in Chicago on July 24th, with stadium shows continuing in the US and Canada through October. Afterwards, Karol G will make her way through Latin America and Europe, with the tour ending in Italy exactly one year after it begins on July 24th, 2027. Learn more details about Karol G’s 2026–2027 “Viajando Por El Mundo Tropitour,” including how to get tickets, below. Tickets for Karol G’s “Viajando Por El Mundo Tropitour” are now available on Ticketmaster. A range of VIP packages — including premium tickets, pre-show VIP Lounge access, and specially designed merch — will be available; check vipnation.com for more information on VIP packages after the tickets go on sale. Fans can also look for deals or get tickets to sold-out shows via StubHub, where orders are 120% guaranteed through StubHub’s FanProtect program. StubHub is a secondary market ticketing platform, and prices may be higher or lower than face value, depending on demand. Additionally, fans can also look for tickets via Ticketmaster’s Verified Resale platform and SeatGeek, where you can use promo code SEATGEEK10 to save $10 off your first order of over $150. The “Viajando Por El Mundo Tropitour” is Karol G’s most ambitious global run to date: a nearly 40-date stadium tour spanning three continents over roughly 14 months. The tour builds on the momentum of her Coachella 2026 headlining set and comes in continued support of Tropicoqueta, her 2025 album and fourth consecutive No. 1 on Billboard’s Top Latin Albums chart. The North American leg launches July 24th at Chicago’s Soldier Field and works its way through stadiums in Toronto, Landover, Las Vegas, Los Angeles, San Francisco, Seattle, Phoenix, San Antonio, El Paso, Boston, East Rutherford, Atlanta, Houston, Miami, Tampa, and Dallas before wrapping October 15th at AT&T Stadium. From there, the tour moves into Latin America: Mexico in November, then Costa Rica, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, Chile, Argentina, Brazil, the Dominican Republic, and Puerto Rico through late February. The European leg opens June 3rd, 2027 in Barcelona, with Karol G set to become the first Latina artist to headline stadiums across the continent as part of a global tour, with stops in Spain, Portugal, France, the United Kingdom, the Netherlands, Poland, Germany, and Italy. Karol G has also promised that more cities will be announced at a future date. title: Becky G Swears By This $0 Under-Eye Hack - bustle.com
content: The singer shares her beauty essentials, including the fragrance that makes her “feel like a boss.” In Bustle’s Beauty Detail, we dive into the beauty bags of our favorite celebrities and talent for intel on the rituals that keep them glowing. Here, Becky G shares her “try it and buy it” approach to beauty, the inspiration behind her new single, and why her signature glam never takes a day off. Twelve years after her breakout single, Becky G is still dancing in the mirror and singing in the shower — razor-sharp liner and all. “I’ve always been a glam girl,” she says. “Even my day-to-day makeup is still very glam.” And yes, that includes at the gym, where she goes “light” with a liquid lipstick, SPF if she’s outside, “and then I definitely got to do the brows,” she laughs. The 29-year-old singer got an early start — not only in her career, which took off when she was a teenager, but also in her beauty mindset. “My mom was a young mom, and it wasn’t like makeup wasn’t allowed,” she says. “She was really aligned with style and makeup being a form of self-expression, so I learned to just have fun with it.” A willingness to try new things has always shaped her approach to beauty. “I’m a big believer in mixing high and low,” she says. “You can’t judge a product just by its price point — I’m definitely a try-it-and-buy-it type of girly when it comes to makeup.” Becky G’s latest music video, though, features a product that’s been a mainstay in her routine for years. In “Marathon,” a Spanglish single, she pulls out Maybelline’s Super Stay Liquid Lipstick alongside the lyric “Pulling off ese Maybelline, who gon’ strut for the world to see?” The song is all about the “mental marathon that is going for your dreams and fighting for them,” she explains — though it can also apply in the literal sense. After partnering up with Maybelline at the Women’s Half Marathon in New York City on April 26 to hand out Super Stay lipsticks to runners, “it’s kind of inspired me to hard-launch my running career,” she jokes — but of course, not without her signature girly-girl glam: airbrushed skin, bright under-eyes, baby pink blush, and cat-eye liner to match. “This face wand cools and depuffs, but it also gets warm, which helps with circulation, especially when I’m traveling a lot. If you have a breakout, the blue light helps clear it up. This was definitely a splurge, but it does work. Confirmed.” “My makeup artist actually put me onto this, and now I love it so much. The clear shade is my go-to — it’s a 10 out of 10. If I’m having a good brow day, I don’t need anything else. But when I’m going for a full beat, I’ll fill in my brows first and then go in with the Super Lock. What’s nice is the applicator is really small, so you can control exactly where you place the hairs.” “I don’t have a signature scent. I have different ones for different occasions, different times of day. I like Freedom Musk Santal for date night because it just melts into the skin. It’s a warm vanilla, musky scent — and I’m a vanilla girl. When I’m going into a big meeting, I want something that feels a little sharper. There’s this Hugo Boss scent — Boss Woman — that makes me feel like a boss.” “I just came back from Korea — it was my first time visiting. I’m really big on skin care, so I was at Olive Young every single day. I love, love, love all the sheet masks. These tea tree oil pads are really good for calming and detoxifying my skin. As somebody who wears makeup pretty often, I think it’s good to have something that can take off all that product build-up.” “I just wore this lipstick in the shade Loyalist to the Billboard Women in Music [event] — it’s a really pretty nude. My makeup artist put me onto this trick: Wear a matte lip as your base, even if you’re adding gloss on top. I layered a gloss over it for my performance because my outfit was sparkly, and my lip combo didn’t budge. My other go-to is a baby pink — [the shade] Dreamer is really cute.” “I bought these in bulk when I was in Korea. I’ll always love a hydrating sheet mask, but don’t underestimate the power of a good old ice cube in the morning. Under the eyes, girl — obviously not for too long and not straight out of the freezer, as that can cause some redness if you have sensitive skin. But gently massaging my under-eyes with ice has helped so much with puffiness, especially when I’m traveling.”

title: Karol G’s Streams Soar Following Historic Coachella Performances - Yahoo
content: Karol G’s Coachella performances are already making an impression on the charts. The Colombian superstar, 35, experienced a strong streaming boost following her historic headlining festival sets. Four songs from her Weekend 1 (April 12) appearance returned to the Hot Latin Songs chart dated April 25, according to Billboard. Karol G’s catalog generated 40.5 million U.S. on-demand official streams during the April 10 to 16 tracking week, up 34 percent from 30.3 million the week prior. Her 2024 single, "Si Antes Te Hubiera Conocido,” leads the returning tracks, reentering at No. 6 with 3.8 million streams, or a 26 percent increase. COMPLEX SHOP: Shop the brands you love, anytime and anywhere. Uncover what's next. Buy. Collect. Obsess. The song previously spent 14 consecutive weeks at No. 1, marking her longest reign among nine chart-toppers and tying with Daniela Romo's "De Mi Enamórate" for the second most weeks at No. 1 by a solo female artist. Mexican pop icon Yuri holds the record with "Qué Te Pasa," which led for 16 weeks in 1988. Among all songs by women, including collaborations, Shakira's "La Tortura" with Alejandro Sanz remains the longest running leader at 25 weeks. Karol G’s "Latina Foreva" from Tropicoqueta jumps back to No. 15 with 2.2 million streams, up 57 percent after its No. 3 peak in June 2025. "Amargura" from 2023’s Mañana Será Bonito returns at No. 19 with 2 million streams, a 34 percent gain, while "Provenza," which reached No. 1 in 2022, reappears at No. 22 with 1.9 million streams, up 29 percent. "Si Antes Te Hubiera Conocido" also is also climbing globally, rising from No. 153 to No. 115 on the Global 200 chart and from No. 124 to No. 109 on the Global Excl. U.S. chart. Additional impact from Karol G’s second Coachella weekend performance (April 19) is expected to appear on the May 1 charts. Following her Coachella performances—which saw her bring out special guests including Wisin, Becky G, Peso Pluma, J Balvin, and Ryan Castro—Karol G announced she will be hitting the road with her Viajando Por El Mundo Tropitour. The tour is scheduled to start in Chicago on July 24 before hitting Las Vegas, Los Angeles, New York, Miami, and more. title: New BMW F 450 GS debuts on track at EICMA Riding Fest - EICMA
content: Some of the data collected by this provider is for the purposes of personalization and measuring advertising effectiveness. BMW Motorrad, among the brands featured at the EICMA Riding Fest 2026 in Misano, is ready to celebrate the dynamic debut of the new F 450 GS adventure bike. Designed specifically for A2 license holders, this new compact adventure model combines accessibility with the authentic GS spirit, powered by a 420 cc parallel-twin engine delivering 48 hp. It features the Easy Ride Clutch and technical solutions directly derived from high-end models. During the Misano event, now in its third edition, the public will have the opportunity to test it through dedicated test rides, evaluating its agility, comfort, and versatility both on-road and off-road. In addition to the F 450 GS, the full BMW Motorrad range will be available, while on the track, riders can get in the saddle of the BMW S 1000 RR for an immersive and thrilling riding experience. © Copyright 2023 Esposizione internazionale del ciclo, motociclo, accessori S.p.A. in sigla E.I.C.M.A. S.p.A - Privacy Policy - Cookie Policy - Condizioni GeneraliSede legale: Via Fabio Filzi 25/a, 20124 Milano - Pi e cf: 04145450153 N° iscrizione 04145450153 - Capitale sociale sottoscritto e versato: 100.000 euroMade by Agenzia YES S.p.A.

title: G.S. Fitness Coordinator Holly Howell completes Al. Triple Crown hike in Gulf Shores & Orange Beach - Mullet Wrapper
content:
The physical nature of the Alabama Triple Crown was of little concern for Holly Howell.
A 20 year resident, she has ably carried the title of fitness coordinator for the City of Gulf Shores for the past 12 years. And she is already an experienced hiker, having climbed several 14K peaks and completed the challenging Four Pass Loop in Colorado.
“I entered a drawing to win a spot and my name was picked,’’ Howell said after completing the Alabama Triple Crown, which consisted of three 20 mile hikes starting in Lineville through the Cheaha Wilderness on the Chin-Pin-Sky Loop Trail, followed by 20 miles on the trails of Oak Mountain State Park and concluding with 20 miles through the Sipsey Wilderness exploring the Bankhead National Forest.
Howell was one of 10 hikers selected to partake in the Challenge, a grassroots effort started by Brad Felton. (His trail name is As The Crow Flies). Nine of the 10 completed the hike modeled after the Lower Michigan Triple Crown.
“I was already an experienced hiker. But as far as Alabama, these were the best trails,’’ Howell said. “I would do it again, for sure.’’
Regarded as a test of physical endurance, the Challenge is designed to push hikers to their limit in a shorter timeframe than long-distance thru-hikes.
“The challenge brought 10 strangers from the Southeast together to form a bond adhered with survival and laughs for miles,’’ Howell said. “I lost count of the number of waterfalls we saw. Alabama is such a beautiful state with incredible hiking trails and diverse ecosystems.’’
The group was helped along the way by a dose of trail magic from volunteers known as trail angels, who, at each day’s halfway mark, fed the hikers sandwiches, salads and hikers trash banana pudding.
“Trail magic along the way reinvigorated us. I wasn’t worried about the physical part at all,’’ Howell said. “It was a fast paced hike. I had no idea it would be that fast. I wondered about callouses and blisters, but everything worked out great. I wore wool socks and had the right shoes and clothes. I was good to go.
“The lack of sleep was the biggest thing. We were on the trail for the most part from dawn ‘til dusk and we were sleeping in tents,’’ she added.
The group had guide help from local hikers while in the Sipsey Wilderness, which lacked marked trails. Howell’s favorite of the three, Sipsey included photo ops with “Big Tree,” Alabama’s tallest and largest Yellow Poplar (150 ft. tall).
Not one to rest on her laurels, Howell followed the Triple Crown by completing a 20 mile hiking adventure inside Oak Mountain State Park just last weekend. She and her husband Lee are already planning a summer trip out west with hikes in the Grand Canyon, Glacier National Park and along the Pacific Coast Hwy.
Howell also leads The City of Gulf Shores Recreation Dept. Off The Beaten Path beginner hiking and camping program, which teaches essential camping and hiking skills and is perfect for those looking to explore the outdoors with confidence.
The group has already taken an overnight trip to Blakely State Park and will take an overnight backpacking trip this fall.
“This is a way for me to carry my love for camping and backpacking into my job and share it with the city,’’ she said.
And yes, Howell has a trail name – Bad Ass Cookie. It is an ode to her grandmother and her grandchildren. (She and Lee have five children and two grandchildren between them).
